name: Bera Holding A.S.
summary: Bera Holding A.S. operates in the paper and cardboard, machinery, oil, construction and building materials, marble, textiles, tourism, and food sectors worldwide. The company manufactures cut and roll paper, school notebooks, box files, drawing papers, office files, printed or plain computer streamline forms, bind or spiral attached notebooks with hard covers, reminder note cubes, parchment papers, packaging papers, A4/A5 blocks, and background cardboard varieties. It is also involved in the production and marketing of bearings; construction of movable and folding hydraulic cranes; provision of agricultural machines; and building of electrical and mechanical bases of construction and infrastructure. In addition, the company engages in the storage of liquid petroleum gas, as well as filling and distribution as bottled gas, flow gas, and car gas; and operation of gas stations and resting facilities. Further, it provides flour, tapioca, dry foods, pasta, biscuits, marshmallows, and wafers under the Golda and Melda brands, as well as bottled spring water; sells and distributes food, hygiene products, and other consumer goods to retailers, wholesalers, hotels, motels, and resorts; and operates hotels. Additionally, the company produces PVC doors and windows, cast concrete, marble, limestone, timber goods, glass panes, and MDF and MDF lamels; polar, velsoft, corduroy, velvet, viscose, and various cotton fabrics; marbles and minerals; and micronized calcide that is used in the production of paper, paint, plastic, glue, ceramic, carpet base, vinyl flooring, and toothpaste products, as well as in the construction, food, and pharmaceutical industries. The company was formerly known as Kombassan Holding A.S. and changed its name to Bera Holding A.S. in July 2017. Bera Holding A.S. was founded in 1988 and is based in Konya, Turkey.
